# services.gnunet

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.gnunet`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.gnunet.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to run the GNUnet daemon. GNUnet is GNU's anonymous peer-to-peer communication and file sharing framework. |
| `services.gnunet.extraOptions` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Additional options that will be copied verbatim in {file}`gnunet.conf`. See {manpage}`gnunet.conf(5)` for details. |
| `services.gnunet.fileSharing.quota` | `signed integer` | Maximum file system usage (in MiB) for file sharing. |
| `services.gnunet.load.hardNetUpBandwidth` | `signed integer` | Hard bandwidth limit (in bits per second) when uploading data. |
| `services.gnunet.load.maxNetDownBandwidth` | `signed integer` | Maximum bandwidth usage (in bits per second) for GNUnet when downloading data. |
| `services.gnunet.load.maxNetUpBandwidth` | `signed integer` | Maximum bandwidth usage (in bits per second) for GNUnet when downloading data. |
| `services.gnunet.package` | `package` | The gnunet package to use. |
| `services.gnunet.tcp.port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | The TCP port for use by GNUnet. |
| `services.gnunet.udp.port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | The UDP port for use by GNUnet. |
